MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT MCLAUGHLIN FAMILY ASSOCIATES, LP, EJC FOLSOM LLC DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to February 1, 2019 for supplemental briefing. No later than January 18, 2019 cross-complainant shall file a supplemental brief limited to:
(1) citation (with a pin cite where necessary) to cases addressing partial performance under factual circumstances similar to the case at bar, i.e. the clause of the agreement pursuant to which improvements are made is optional/not required to be performed by the contract such as section 4.3. Only citation without any argument shall be provided. If any argument is included, the supplemental brief will be stricken in its entirety. If no such cases can be cited, response should state "N/A";
(2) facts (who, to whom, when, in what manner, under what circumstances) that could be alleged in good faith regarding the following assertion in paragraph 24: "McLauhlin requested, and 620 Folsom agreed, that signatures would be exchanged after the holiday; only proposed facts shall be stated. If no facts could be alleged in good faith, the brief should state "No facts." No argument is authorized. If any argument is included, the supplemental brief will be stricken in its entirety; and
(3) facts that could be alleged in good faith showing what was the parties' understanding of the term "after the holiday," i.e. what date is that, e.g. January 2, 2018 or March 22, 2018 or December 17, 2018. If no facts could be alleged in good faith, the brief should state "No facts." No argument is authorized. If any argument is included, the supplemental brief will be stricken in its entirety.
By no later than January 25, 2019 Cross-Defendant may file a supplemental brief addressing only Cross-Complainant's response to the first point listed above. Response should be limited to citations (with pin cites where appropriate) to cases stating for the opposition proposition to any cases cited by the Cross-Complainant. Only citation without any argument shall be provided. If any argument is included, the supplemental brief will be stricken in its entirety. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported.
